task_completion.description: Verify that the 'narration.wav' audio file has been imported into the session.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an audio region or clip named 'narration' or 'narration.wav' visible in the main timeline or editor window of the DAW (Ardour).

task_completion.description: Verify that the track has been renamed to 'Phone Futz'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the track header on the left side of the timeline or in the mixer window. The track name should be 'Phone Futz' (or contain the keywords 'phone', 'futz', or 'telephone').

task_completion.description: Verify that the track gain is set to approximately -6.0 dB.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the mixer strip or track header for the 'Phone Futz' track. The fader level or the numerical gain readout should show a value around -6.0 dB (any value between -9.0 dB and -3.0 dB is acceptable).

task_completion.description: Verify that a High-Pass Filter (HPF) is applied at approximately 400 Hz.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an open EQ plugin window (e.g., a-EQ, ACE EQ) inserted on the track. A high-pass filter band (cutting low frequencies) should be enabled, with its frequency parameter set near 400 Hz (acceptable range: 250 Hz to 600 Hz).

task_completion.description: Verify that a Low-Pass Filter (LPF) is applied at approximately 4000 Hz.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the same EQ plugin window. A low-pass filter band (cutting high frequencies) should be enabled, with its frequency parameter set near 4000 Hz (acceptable range: 2500 Hz to 5000 Hz).

task_completion.description: Verify that the session has been saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the application window title bar. There should be no asterisk (*) next to the project name, which would indicate unsaved changes. Alternatively, look for a 'Saved' message in the status bar.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the DAW graphical user interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the Ardour GUI is open and was interacted with. The agent should not be simply editing the .ardour XML session file in a text editor or terminal.

integrity.description: Ensure the EQ plugin is actually inserted into the track's signal flow.
integrity.visual_evidence: The EQ plugin window should clearly belong to the 'Phone Futz' track (e.g., the plugin name should be visible in the track's mixer strip processor box), rather than being a standalone application disconnected from the session.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not use terminal scripts to bypass manual GUI configuration.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for terminal windows where python, bash, or sed commands might have been used to directly inject the EQ settings or gain values into the session file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Track name must contain keywords: 'phone', 'futz', or 'telephone'. Track gain must be -6.0 dB (acceptable tolerance range: -9.0 dB to -3.0 dB). High-pass filter (HPF) frequency must be 400 Hz (acceptable range: 250 Hz to 600 Hz). Low-pass filter (LPF) frequency must be 4000 Hz (acceptable range: 2500 Hz to 5000 Hz).
